text: Add release / publish workflow (PyPI, GitHub Releases) once a 1.0 release is ready. Out of scope for 011-S which is CI-gate bootstrap only.

[review follow-up from 019-F] Validate weights_path in fidelity_scorer.load_weights against workspace containment when the path is supplied by an untrusted source (e.g. an MCP server caller). Today the CLI is the only caller and operator-supplied paths are trusted, so this is low priority. Becomes relevant when ProcessRequest is exposed via the MCP server and pdf_mode=triage is invoked remotely with a caller-controlled weights JSON path. Mitigation: reuse safe_workspace_path / validate_workspace_relative_path from docline.paths to constrain the loaded file under the workspace root.

Verify extraction-strategy study generalizes: re-run scripts/study pipeline against 2-3 additional corpus classes (scientific paper, legal contract, novel/article-style). Confirms or refutes the universal docling-wins finding from the cosmos-only 2026-06-08 study. Use the same stratified sampling + AST metrics. Reference: docs/decisions/2026-06-08-extraction-strategy-study.md

023-F strategic: multi-repo corpus orchestration. Staging mode that ingests N sibling docs repos with inter-repo link resolution. Example: [text](/fabric/admin/foo) link from powerbi-docs/connect-data/bar.md resolves to fabric-docs target. Enables full Power BI + Fabric + DAX + Analysis Services graph in one ingest pass. Prereqs: local-dir fetch source type (separate stash), cross-product link extraction (separate stash). Use case: operator wants to ingest powerbi-docs + fabric-docs + dax-docs + analysis-services-docs (~3,600 additional PDF-page-equivalents) as one connected graph. See docs/decisions/2026-06-09-powerbi-corpus-coverage.md "Recommended sibling repos" section.

SEPARATE PIPELINE: OpenAPI / Swagger source type. For REST API reference content (1,686-page rest-api-power-bi.pdf, equivalent docs for other Microsoft REST APIs). Canonical source is Azure/azure-rest-api-specs OpenAPI files. Different ingestion model than source-MD or PDF — operation summaries, parameter schemas, response shapes, auth requirements. Needs its own design doc and dedicated shipment. Out of scope for 023-F source-MD work. See docs/decisions/2026-06-09-powerbi-corpus-coverage.md "Recommended sibling repos" section.

[REFRAMED post-031-S 2026-06-13] Re-validate **Mistral OCR** on forms / invoices corpus class. The 2026-06-12 ADI study (docs/closure/029-S-adi-spike.md) and 2026-06-13 Mistral study (docs/closure/031-S-mistral-ocr-spike.md) both used cosmos-class technical reference PDFs. Mistral PROMOTE-AS-PEER for technical refs (tables +33.9% vs docling) but corpus fitness on forms/invoices is unverified. Re-run scripts/study/mistral_comparison.py against a sample forms corpus (IRS tax forms, vendor invoice scans). Confirm/refute Mistral's strength on form-class content. If Mistral wins or loses by wide margin, document the corpus-class fitness guidance in README + ARCHITECTURE.md. ADI is no longer in the codebase as of 031-S, so this is now a 2-way comparison: Mistral OCR vs docling on forms.

[follow-up from 032-S T1+T2] Per-page docling fidelity restoration spike. Currently, multi-page splice ranges produce `engine="docling-collapsed"` attribution because `_read_pdf_docling_pages` returns docling's single concatenated markdown blob (one call per splice). However, `DocumentConverter.convert()` exposes a `page_range: Tuple[int,int]` parameter. Under 032-S T3's batched-worker mode (docling model loaded once per subprocess), looping `page_range=(i,i)` per page within a chunk would give true per-page fidelity at only per-page inference cost (no cold-start). Contingent on T4 probe result: if per-page-loop overhead < 2× single multi-page call, ship Option 2 inside the batched worker and retire the "docling-collapsed" attribution entirely. References: docs/closure/032-S-docling-worker-contract.md "What did NOT ship", deliberation 001-DL Option 2.

[follow-up from 032-S T3] Multi-chunk-per-file envelope shape evolution. Deferred from deliberation 001-DL. Currently each chunk in batched mode gets its own envelope file (per-chunk path-routing preserved). An evolution could consolidate N chunks into one file with `{"chunks": {"<id>": {"pages": [...]}}}` shape for very large N. Low priority — only worth doing if per-chunk file I/O becomes a measurable hot path on production runs (would require per-file mode-switch in pdf_batch and pdf_triage consumers). Bump envelope schema_version to 2 on adoption.

[follow-up from 032-S T2] Per-page H1 markers in pdf_batch stitcher. HTML-comment markers like `<!-- page 5 -->` injected at page boundaries during `_stitch_chunk_markdown` so downstream graph writers can recover page boundaries from the stitched markdown. Low priority — gated on downstream consumer needs (graphtor-docs ingestion is the likely first consumer). References: src/docline/process/pdf_batch.py _stitch_chunk_markdown.

[follow-up from 032-S out-of-scope] GPU acceleration spike for docling. docling's DocumentConverter can run on CUDA/MPS. Needs accelerator detection (already partial in src/docline/runtime/resource_probe.py), DocConverter pipeline_options configuration, env-var gating, and benchmark vs CPU baseline. Could unlock another large throughput win on GPU-equipped hosts (cosmos PA3+PA4 baseline was 247min on CPU). References: docling.datamodel.pipeline_options accelerator field.

[SPIKE candidate, follow-up from 031-S + 032-S] Hybrid pdf_engine mode that routes specific pages to Mistral OCR based on per-page signals, when Foundry credentials are available. EMPIRICAL JUSTIFICATION (031-S verdict PROMOTE-AS-PEER): Mistral wins decisively on tables (+33.9% chars, 8/4/2 wins) and has ~10x throughput; docling wins on heading depth (-8.4% for Mistral). The asymmetry per-page suggests page-class-based routing could beat either engine alone. PROPOSED MECHANISM: (1) Triage scorer (028-F) already computes per-page signals — extend with mistral_preference scoring that flags pages where table_density and structural complexity indicate a likely Mistral win. (2) Coalesce flagged pages into ranges (already done). (3) Per range, the router applies a rule: if range-dominant signal == "table_heavy" AND AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_KEY in env → route to Mistral reader; else → route to docling worker. (4) Both engines produce per-page envelopes (Mistral reader adopts the 032-S T1 envelope shape for uniform splice-back). (5) Per-engine attribution: "docling", "mistral", "docling-collapsed", "mistral-collapsed", "heuristic". NEW MODE: --pdf-engine=hybrid (4th choice alongside auto, docling, mistral_ocr, heuristic). Auto-policy decision: hybrid stays opt-in initially; if spike shows ≥10% combined-fidelity win without cost regression, consider auto-promotion when Foundry creds detected. SPIKE SCOPE: (a) implement prototype router (engine_per_range field on TriageResult before splice); (b) extend Mistral reader to optionally emit envelopes matching 032-S T1 shape; (c) run cosmos sample through docling-only, mistral-only, hybrid; compare AST-aware metrics across the three; record $ cost; record wall-clock; (d) write decision artifact with ADOPT-HYBRID / KEEP-AS-OPT-IN / ABANDON verdict. PREREQUISITES (all already in place or pending merge): 028-F triage scorer (shipped); 031-S Mistral reader (shipped); 032-S envelope contract (PR #85 pending merge — gives uniform per-page output across engines); MISTRAL_OCR_MODEL env var for model selection (shipped in 031-S). REFERENCES: docs/closure/031-S-mistral-ocr-spike.md (empirical asymmetry data), docs/closure/032-S-docling-worker-contract.md (envelope contract), src/docline/process/fidelity_scorer.py (signal computation), src/docline/readers/mistral.py (Mistral reader), src/docline/process/pdf_triage.py (splice-back where router would live). RISK NOTES: Mistral 30-page per-request limit means range coalescing must keep ranges ≤30 pages (already a hard limit anyway). Cost regression possible if router over-flags pages. Cred-availability gate prevents auto-routing in setups without Foundry. Decision needs corpus generalization beyond cosmos (already a separate stash entry for that).

[review F4 from 036-S] Unify _range_needs_ocr (src/docline/process/pdf_triage.py) and _chunk_needs_ocr (src/docline/process/pdf_batch.py). Both run the same per-page "extract text (pypdf) -> fidelity_scorer.page_needs_ocr -> any() short-circuit" loop, just over different inputs (a pypdf reader + page range vs a chunk PDF path). Extract a shared helper that takes an iterable of (text, page_metadata) pairs (or a pypdf reader + index range) so the OCR-gate logic lives in one place. Low priority maintainability dedup surfaced by the 036-S adversarial review; no behavior change.

Stop treating the docling whole-range render as an anomaly: silence the 'envelope length mismatch' WARNING spam and fix the misleading docling-collapsed attribution metric. CONTEXT/REFRAME (2026-06-28): originally filed high as a per-page fidelity regression, but analysis shows per-page fidelity does NOT affect product outcomes and a per-page split would HARM them. Evidence chain: the final markdown is output_contract.py:264 rendered = '\\n\\n'.join(p for p in triage_result.pages if p.strip()) -- empty page-slots are dropped and page boundaries erased before ingestion; heading validation + anchor injection run on the WHOLE joined body (assemble.py:141-144 validate_heading_hierarchy(body)), not per page. So whether a docling range sits in N page-slots or collapses onto 1 slot, the assembled markdown graphtor-docs ingests is identical text in identical document order. The collapse branch (pdf_triage.py:692-706) already uses the envelope 'text' field = '\\n\\n'.join(pages) = docling's single COHERENT whole-range export_to_markdown(), which preserves heading nesting and tables/lists across page breaks. A per-page export_to_markdown(page_no=N) split (the originally-proposed fix) would FRAGMENT those structures at page boundaries -> worse AST/heading/lint. The 030-F per-page envelope contract was the wrong contract for a whole-document-AST consumer. WHAT THIS COSTS NOW: (1) thousands of WARNING log lines (operational noise); (2) inaccurate INTERNAL routing metric (docling-collapsed / content_pages) that misleads our own analysis (the 82%-docling investigation) but is invisible to the product. NOT markdown quality, AST, heading hierarchy, or node referentiality. FIX DIRECTION (do NOT do a per-page split): in pdf_triage.py splice-back, treat a single-entry envelope for an N-page range as the EXPECTED whole-range render -- assign the coherent blob to the range, drop the mismatch WARNING, and relabel attribution (e.g. 'docling-range' instead of pejorative 'docling-collapsed'); keep collapsed pages out of the per-page count or report ranges, not pages, in docling_attribution. Files: src/docline/process/pdf_triage.py (splice-back + attribution), possibly the docling_attribution summary in scripts/pa3_triage_cosmos.py. Refs: docs/decisions/2026-06-28-merge-gap-tuning-verdict.md; output_contract.py:264; assemble.py:141-144.

Batched docling worker OCR OOM crash (access violation) on image pages. During the 036.002-T cosmos sweep (mg1 run, 2026-06-27) a batched docling group crashed hard (exit=3221225477 = 0xC0000005 access violation) while RapidOCR ran: std::bad_alloc, onnxruntime 'Non-zero status code ... bad allocation' on Clip node, numpy _ArrayMemoryError unable to allocate 30.7 MiB / 1.92 MiB. The whole group died and fell back to heuristic (subprocess_fallback_count=1), contaminating that run's wall-clock. Two concerns: (1) OCR ran at all on a native-text Azure-docs PDF, so 036-S conditional-OCR (skip OCR on native-text pages) may not be applied in the batched-worker path; (2) the batched worker loads too many page bitmaps per group and exhausts memory with no per-group bound. Fix: verify conditional-OCR gating applies to the batched path and bound per-group bitmap memory / cap pages-per-group under memory pressure. Files: src/docline/process/pdf_triage.py (batched worker), src/docline/process/pdf_batch.py. Refs: docs/decisions/2026-06-28-merge-gap-tuning-verdict.md, .elt/output/results.txt lines ~1448-1582.
